meta.source: The Depository Trust Company Operational Arrangements
meta.note: Single teaching canon for timelines, LOR/CUSIP rules, and legacy form deltas. Not legal advice.

timelines.label: UW SOURCE / UWC eligibility package
timelines.legacyForm: ≥ 10 business days pre-close questionnaire + prospectus; CUSIP 7 BD / corp upon pricing
timelines.oaRule: ≥ 6 business days prior to Closing Date via UW SOURCE or UWC (offering document, lead underwriter, CUSIP(s), principal/share amounts, rates/maturities as applicable)

timelines.label: BEO LOR — U.S. issuers
timelines.legacyForm: Often taught as 1 BD pre-close with securities
timelines.oaRule: Final executed PDF of BLOR/ILOR at least 3 business days prior to closing

timelines.label: BEO LOR — Non-U.S. issuers
timelines.oaRule: Draft LOR ≥ 10 business days prior; original signed hard copy by 3 business days prior

timelines.label: Securities / FRAC for inspection
timelines.legacyForm: 1 BD pre-close by noon ET (LOR + securities bundled)
timelines.oaRule: Physical certificate for inspection by noon ET business day before closing, or Agent FRAC FAST balance confirmation by noon ET on Closing Date

timelines.label: Closing notification
timelines.oaRule: Underwriter and Agent notify DTC of closing by 1:15 p.m. ET on Closing Date (≥ $100M extension requests by 1:00 p.m. ET)

timelines.label: Agent Confirmation (features/attributes)
timelines.legacyForm: Sometimes confused with Agent Letter or pre-close delivery
timelines.oaRule: Within 10 business days after Closing Date, Agent emails confirmation of securities features/attributes for UW SOURCE/UWC issues

timelines.label: Operational Arrangements Agent Letter
timelines.oaRule: Agent must have completed Agent Letter on file with DTC before the security becomes eligible

rules.label: Submission channel
rules.summary: Full-service eligibility requests must go through UW SOURCE or Underwriting Central (UWC) at a minimum.

rules.label: Rule 144A / Reg S CUSIP
rules.summary: Restricted securities must carry a distinct CUSIP/CINS from unrestricted securities of the same class.

rules.label: Required LOR riders
rules.summary: Riders required for: (1) older 144A and Reg S issues, (2) non-U.S. currency denomination/payments, (3) each Ireland or U.K. issuer security. List all CUSIPs on each rider.

rules.label: Serial / term CUSIPs
rules.summary: Each municipal serial or term maturity needs its own CUSIP (and table row).

rules.label: Certificate sizing
rules.summary: If a maturity exceeds $500 million, multiple certificates are required (one per $500M tranche).

rules.label: OFAC
rules.summary: Issuers, Agents, and Underwriters must not violate OFAC. SDN/sanctioned issuers are not DTC eligible. Participants screen; transfer-agent Agents provide OFAC certifications.

rules.label: NIIDS (municipals)
rules.summary: Municipal trade/settlement data dissemination via UW SOURCE is separate from the eligibility request. Completing NIIDS ≠ completing eligibility.

rules.label: Standards vs deadlines
rules.summary: Times applicable to DTC are standards, not hard deadlines; DTC may extend timeframes. Still plan to published Exhibit B standards; late filings can draw surcharges.

rules.label: PORTAL (legacy)
rules.summary: PORTAL is retired. Distinct restricted CUSIP remains mandatory for 144A; no PORTAL eligibility step.

rules.label: Underwriting customer help
rules.summary: OA publishes (866) 724-4402 for MMI/E-CD and general underwriting questions. 2000 form 212-855-xxxx numbers are legacy facsimile only.

crosswalk.was: ≥ 10 BD questionnaire + prospectus; CUSIP 7 BD / corp upon pricing
Often taught as ≥ 3 BD prior to closing
crosswalk.now: Within 10 BD after Closing Date (email) for UW SOURCE/UWC issues
U.S. final PDF ≥ 3 BD; non-U.S. draft ≥ 10 BD, original by 3 BD
Required for non-U.S. currency denomination/payments (§ I.B.1.b)
Expressly required for older issues; distinct CUSIP always (§ II.A.1.b)
Form language is for certificated put/tender; BEO needs separate confirmation
OA applies with MMI-specific Rules/guides; help 866-724-4402
Separate from eligibility; Exhibit B Note 2 + § I.C.3
Legacy 2000 facsimile; prefer OA 866-724-4402 / Client Center

legacyFacsimile.note: 2000 questionnaire 212-855-xxxx extensions are historical only — verify before calling.
